name: pyproj

performance.notes: Sub-millisecond coordinate transformations; PROJ C library bindings

agent_readiness.notes: Python interface to the PROJ library for cartographic projections and coordinate transformations. Transformer class for converting between CRS (WGS84 ↔ UTM, state plane, custom projections). CRS class for inspecting and creating coordinate reference systems. Integrated into GeoPandas, Rasterio, Shapely. Essential for any Python geospatial work involving coordinate reprojection.

gotchas: CRS authority (EPSG, ESRI, PROJ) differences cause confusion
always_xy parameter needed for consistent (lon, lat) vs (lat, lon) axis ordering
PROJ network access for datum grids — may fail in air-gapped environments
CRS string formats (EPSG, WKT, PROJ string) all supported but can confuse users
best_when: Coordinate reprojection between any CRS (EPSG:4326 → EPSG:32633 etc.)
Geographic calculations (geodesic distance, area) on the ellipsoid
Supporting other geospatial libraries needing CRS management
Building geospatial tools requiring robust coordinate system handling
avoid_when: Simple lat/lon distance calculations (use haversine formula directly)
If GeoPandas or Shapely already handle your CRS needs transparently
summary: pyproj is the Python interface to the PROJ cartographic projection library. Provides the Transformer class for coordinate reprojection between any CRS, and the CRS class for coordinate reference system management. Sub-millisecond transformations. Integrated into GeoPandas, Rasterio, and Shapely. Essential dependency for any Python geospatial work requiring CRS conversions.
